title: Update README, spec, configuration docs, and CHANGELOG

paradigm.rationale: Documentation-only story: human-facing description of the harness concept, the --harness flag, the opencode framework, the harness ⇒ framework rule, and the boy-scout USE_BUILTIN_RIPGREP=0 removal. Acts as the epic-wide test gate — AC-024 forces make check to remain green and coverage ≥80% AT this story boundary, which means earlier stories' tests must coexist cleanly. AC-025 requires grep audits of the documentation surfaces.

components.name: README.md frameworks table extension
components.responsibility: Add codex and opencode rows so the table truthfully describes the supported frameworks. Codex was added in a previous epic and is currently absent from this table — boy-scout fix to land alongside the opencode addition.
components.rationale: AC-025 grep audit checks that 'opencode' appears in README.md. The table is the natural place. Adding the missing codex row in the same edit is a tiny boy-scout per CLAUDE.md.

components.name: README.md 'Choosing a harness' subsection
components.location: README.md, after the existing frameworks/configuration content (around §Configuration or as a peer to existing usage subsections)
components.responsibility: Explain the three harness options (claude-code, codex, opencode), the --harness flag (override-only), and the wizard step. Show the example invocation `clauded --harness opencode` and `clauded --edit`. Note that the harness is project-local (.clauded.yaml carries the value).
components.rationale: User-facing landing page must teach the new concept; AC-025 grep also matches 'harness' here.

components.name: specs/spec.md harness model documentation
components.location: specs/spec.md — Configuration Management section + CLI Interface section + relevant frameworks list
components.responsibility: Add 'opencode' to runtime/framework lists, document the harness config field (allowed values, default), the --harness flag (override-only semantics; ADR-001), and the harness ⇒ framework validation rule (FR4).
components.constraint: Per project CLAUDE.md (Documentation Guidelines): keep specs/spec.md focused on what/why; no concrete file paths or code snippets. ADR-001 in this codebase's specs/spec.md history refers to the override-only design decision documented in spec.md of the epic, not to a separate ADR file. Reference the high-level requirement, not the implementation.

components.name: docs/configuration.md 'Choosing a harness' section
components.location: docs/configuration.md — new section after the existing 'Claude Code Permissions' section but before 'Minimal Configuration'
components.responsibility: Document harness config field with the FR7 dispatcher matrix (claude-code | codex | opencode → command construction → permission flag). Show an example .clauded.yaml snippet with harness: opencode, frameworks containing opencode, and optional versions.opencode pin. Provide a brief troubleshooting note for the harness ⇒ framework error (with the exact 'clauded --edit' fix). Update the frameworks section to include codex and opencode entries; remove the stale 'USE_BUILTIN_RIPGREP=0' bullet from the claude-code framework entry (boy-scout — Story 04 removed that env var).

components.name: CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] consolidation
components.responsibility: Verify the existing Stories-01-05 entries (added by each architect in their story PR) are correctly bucketed under Added / Changed / Removed. Add Story 06's own entry under Changed for the documentation refresh. DO NOT release-tag — Story 07 is deferred per spec Implementation Decisions.
components.constraint: DO NOT delete or rewrite earlier stories' entries. Add a Story-06 documentation-update entry only.

components.name: tests/test_documentation.py grep audit
components.responsibility: Lock in AC-025 mechanically via grep-based assertions: the three required terms (opencode, harness, --harness) appear at least once in each of README.md, specs/spec.md, docs/configuration.md, CHANGELOG.md. Plus a negative assertion that 'USE_BUILTIN_RIPGREP' no longer appears in any non-CHANGELOG doc (the CHANGELOG correctly mentions the removal). Plus a verification-plan-step-4 negative grep: 'opencode-alpine' must NOT appear anywhere under src/.
components.rationale: AC-025 says 'Manual review; grep for harness in those files yields the expected coverage.' A test that runs that grep at make-check time turns the manual check into a regression-resistant invariant. Story-06-only test; no production code changes.

boundary_rules_compliance: Rule 2 (Config single source of truth): n/a.
Rule 4 (no test-only adaptations): the new test_documentation.py asserts properties of the docs themselves, not test-only production code. Compliant.
Rule 5 (fragile seam coordination): n/a — no shared list duplications touched.

Rule 7 (boy-scout USE_BUILTIN_RIPGREP=0 removal): Story 04 already removed it from production code; Story 06 removes the stale documentation reference (boy-scout doc cleanup, in-scope for the documentation pass).
implementation_constraints_compliance: Pre-Alpine-removal targeting: docs may still reference Alpine; not changed in this epic (alpine-removal is a separate epic).
Coverage ≥ 80%: AC-024 hard requirement. Verified at story boundary by `uv run pytest --cov=clauded`.
CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased]: Story 06's contribution is a Changed entry for the documentation refresh. Earlier stories' entries are preserved.
Backward compatibility: docs describe pre-epic configs as still loading cleanly (default harness=claude-code).
Each story leaves codebase green: enforced by make check.

patterns_to_reuse: plain Path-based file reads + substring/regex assertions

new_test_cases.name: test_readme_mentions_opencode_harness_and_flag
new_test_cases.shape: Read README.md; assert 'opencode' substring + 'harness' substring + '--harness' substring all present.

new_test_cases.name: test_changelog_mentions_opencode_harness_and_flag
new_test_cases.shape: Read CHANGELOG.md; assert 'opencode' + 'harness' + '--harness' all present in the [Unreleased] section.

new_test_cases.name: test_no_use_builtin_ripgrep_in_user_facing_docs
new_test_cases.shape: Read README.md, docs/configuration.md, specs/spec.md; assert 'USE_BUILTIN_RIPGREP' substring is absent. (CHANGELOG.md is excluded — it correctly mentions the removal.)

new_test_cases.name: test_no_opencode_alpine_in_src
new_test_cases.shape: Walk src/; assert no file contains 'opencode-alpine'.

new_test_cases.name: test_changelog_unreleased_not_empty
new_test_cases.shape: Parse CHANGELOG.md; assert the [Unreleased] section contains at least one of Added/Changed/Removed/Fixed/Deprecated/Security with non-empty content.

patterns_to_avoid: Do NOT create temporary docs files.
Do NOT commit a /docs/* changelog drift — single CHANGELOG.md at repo root.

implementation_plan_outline: Step 1 (TDD red, docs): write tests/test_documentation.py with grep-based assertions covering AC-025 and the boy-scout no-USE_BUILTIN_RIPGREP-in-docs invariant. Run pytest; confirm failures (the target strings will be missing or stale).
Step 2 (CHANGELOG): inspect existing [Unreleased] entries from Stories 01-05; verify bucketing; add a single Story 06 Changed entry for the documentation refresh.

Step 3 (docs/configuration.md): add a 'Choosing a harness' section with the dispatcher matrix and example .clauded.yaml. Update the frameworks section to add codex and opencode entries; remove the stale 'USE_BUILTIN_RIPGREP=0' bullet from claude-code.

Step 4 (specs/spec.md): add 'opencode' to the frameworks list in the Module Responsibilities and Config Schema sections; document the harness field (allowed values + default); document the --harness flag in the CLI Options; document FR4 (harness ⇒ framework). Per CLAUDE.md, keep it high-level.
Step 5 (README.md): add codex and opencode rows to the frameworks table; add a 'Choosing a harness' subsection with the canonical examples.
Step 6 (gate): make check + pytest --cov; confirm all tests pass and coverage ≥ 80%.
Step 7 (cleanup): no temp files; story directory contains only architecture.json, baseline.json, verification.json, result.json.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Adding codex rows to the README frameworks table is technically scope-creep relative to AC-025 (which only requires opencode/harness/flag mentions).
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: CLAUDE.md authorizes the boy-scout rule for small adjacent issues spotted in passing. The existing table is misleading without codex (it has been a framework for a previous epic). One row addition is appropriate scope; not expanding into a refactor.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: specs/spec.md edits can drift into implementation detail (file paths, code snippets) — explicitly forbidden by project CLAUDE.md.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Story 06 architecture pre-commits to high-level wording only. The new content names the field, allowed values, the flag, and the validation rule; it does NOT name file paths, function names, or code excerpts.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: The grep-based audit test is brittle if a future doc renames 'opencode' to e.g. 'OpenCode'.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Use case-insensitive substring matching ('opencode' lower-case is the canonical brand spelling per the spec; Title-case mentions are stylistic rephrasings of the same name). Asserting case-insensitive '.lower()' coverage is acceptable for the audit.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Coverage might tip below 80% if the new test_documentation.py file exposes uncovered branches in the docs-related code (there is none today, but caution is warranted).
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: test_documentation.py has no production-code dependencies; it walks doc files directly. It cannot reduce coverage; it can only increase the test count.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: An unrelated PR may land [Unreleased] entries between Story 05 and Story 06 (per stories.json's noted edge case).
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: The CHANGELOG audit test simply checks the [Unreleased] section is non-empty and mentions the harness. Unrelated entries do not invalidate Story 06 — they are simply additional content.

ac_to_artifact.AC-024: make check exit 0 + pytest --cov reports total ≥ 80%

out_of_scope_explicitly: Code changes (no production code touched in this story).
New test coverage of code paths (only doc-audit tests added).
End-to-end VM provisioning validation (Story 07, deferred manual step).
Architecture docs in docs/architecture.md (out of FR12 scope; specs/spec.md is the canonical architecture surface for this epic).
